briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — aggressive fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'Fall dirty water after heavy rain — head farther back in creeks into the dirty water. Incoming stained water pulls shad toward current and bass follow. Fish get extremely shallow and tight to co
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: The importance of showing bass something they haven't seen or heard — a squarebill that combines attributes of a bladed jig (oscillating metal lip with clicking sound) and a crankbait body covers water and triggers reaction strikes

KVD: Square bills are round-bodied with a steep angle and a square bill — this gives them a wide wobble and the best deflection qualities of any crankbait. Most weedless crankbait you can throw. Good through grass, brush, gnarly rocks, riprap. Wor...

Robertson: Spring crankbait progression -- 1) Prespawn: slow-roll lipless on flats near channel banks in 1-6 feet; 2) Postspawn: big-bodied squarebill on tapering points near spawning flats in under 10 feet; 3) Late postspawn: deep diver following...

Palaniuk: 'For southern regions the key player is the shad — I lean towards colors with more flash, silver or white hues with a touch of color. For northern regions, fish follow perch on outside weedlines and transitions to deep water, so I go mor...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
